title: Avoid defining environment variables from untrusted input
description: Defining environment variables using untrusted input can introduce severe security vulnerabilities. Attackers may manipulate these variables to alter application behavior or gain unauthorized access. Always validate and sanitize any input used to set environment variables.
recommendation: Never use untrusted or user-controlled input to define environment variables. Validate and sanitize all sources before use.

checks.message: Never use untrusted or user-controlled input to define environment variables. Validate and sanitize all sources before use.
